title: Social Norm Tipping Point
description: How does a minority opinion become the majority norm? Watch the threshold at which preference cascades become inevitable.

initial_adopters.label: Initial Adopters
initial_adopters.description: Fraction who start with the new norm

conformity_threshold.label: Conformity Threshold
conformity_threshold.description: Fraction needed before others switch (lower = faster cascade)

narration.text: Teal = people with the new behaviour/belief. Grey = people following the old norm. The new norm starts as a small minority.
Early adopters influence those nearby. Each convert is one more signal to the undecided: 'this is becoming normal'.
The cascade begins. Once enough people adopt the new norm, the social pressure overwhelms the cost of switching.
Notice the wave — it doesn't spread uniformly. It spreads faster where adopters are clustered, slower at the edges of the group.
The tipping point is behind us. The minority has become the majority. The old norm is now the deviant behaviour.
This pattern governs: smoking becoming socially unacceptable, seatbelt adoption, marriage equality, mask-wearing. Small committed minorities + social proof = tipping point.
